Who We ArePwC Services Portugal (PSP) is a joint venture between PwC Luxembourg and PwC Portugal, serving as an integral extension of PwC Luxembourg. Our growing team operates as an acceleration center supporting PwC Luxembourg’s clients. Knowledge of French or German would be considered an asset. …You are the candidate we are looking for!

Job SummaryJob Description Group Internal Audit (GIA) is looking for an experienced and dynamic audit professional with a solid background and strong knowledge around the key risks and controls in the Finance, Property and Treasury functions. The role-holder will be an integral part of the Finance, Property (East) and Treasury (East) Audit team. The team provides independent assessments of risk management and internal control environment of the Finance, Property and Treasury functions globally. We pride ourselves in being auditors of the future, forward-looking and agile with a keen focus on smarter auditing. When you join us, you will enter a role that challenges the status quo and moves mindsets and be a part of the future of banking. This role currently reports to Head of Audit Vietnam, Australia and Finance, Property (East) and Treasury (East).Key Responsibilities To act as Team Lead/ Team Member, as necessary, and take responsibility for contributing to the delivery of high-quality Finance, Property and Treasury function audits, the performance of which cause minimal disruption to the business. To role model the valued behaviours and develop an environment in which positive behaviours are celebrated, and poor culture is challenged. To execute assigned audit work, as well as the work carried out by the team, in an efficient and effective manner, within the given budget and timelines, and in line with GIA methodology standards. To clearly identify the risks and impact of issues during issue writing, agreeing these issues with management and obtaining quality management action plans to mitigate the risks raised. To support GIA audit teams by providing product knowledge and expertise for their audits relating to the individual’s area of responsibility. Issue validation: All audit issue action plans agreed during audit fieldwork should be tracked through to completion in accordance with methodology requirements. To attend Bangladesh Finance Operational Team Meeting’ (FORTM) and ‘Transformation, Technology and Operations Non-Financial Risk Forum’ (TTO NFRF) committee meeting. Review of AML self-assessment prepared by branches/units and Self-Assessment of Anti-Fraud Internal Controls Report to Bangladesh Bank, To prepare and provide information for various local regulatory reports (e.g. MRMR/CRMR, CAMEL information, BASEL III/ICAAP information, Update of NFRR/NEWS, BIA/BCP/DR, Annual Health Report, DBI 4 & 32, Risk Based Supervision (RBS) information,Strategy Audits assigned to the individual in the GIA audit plan should address the key risks identified in the detailed risk assessment and in the audit planning process and meet relevant regulatory requirements and expectations that are required to be covered by GIA. Ensure that audit team operates in line with the Audit Charter during engagements, remains independent from management and free from interference.Business Effectively manage the cost of assigned audits within the allocated budget for their areas within audit engagementsProcesses To act as Team Lead/ Team Member on assigned audit work across the Group. When Team Leading to provide clear guidance, detailed review and supervision of the audit team’s work so that audit deliverables meet quality standards and timelines in line with the GIA methodology. When Team Leading to provide guidance on business/audit technical knowledge and management skills to team members to enable them to effectively deliver their assigned contributions for an audit. Provide technical input and challenge on audit work being undertaken within the scope of assigned product area of responsibility. This will include working with the audit team to produce outputs of high quality which address the areas of greatest risk. Support adherence to the GIA methodology in all areas of the audit engagement, as well as raising awareness and understanding of the methodology. Demonstrate sound knowledge of both business/technical areas and expert knowledge in the audit process, including the GIA system, so that audit work is carried out to a high standard that meets all methodology and GIA system requirements. Monitor the implementation/delivery of the agreed issues/audit plans for the audits assigned, understanding the key risks arising, provide advice on resolution of issues to auditees/action plan owners and escalate audit findings that remain unresolved.People & Talent Share knowledge to increase specialism within the function. Influence change within the department by highlighting potential enhancements. Identify growth areas on an ongoing basis and identify how best to develop them. When Team leading provide performance feedback, as appropriate at the end of an audit. Identify and successfully complete key internal training for self-development. Facilitate the development of audit team members by providing on the job training and recommending formal training to support GIA activitiesRisk Management Assist in the update of the assigned Con

Proven experience managing external audits and delivering accurate, timely financial statements. Proficiency in financial systems, ERP platforms and digital finance transformation initiatives.

location: Santa Maria da Feira, Aveiro, Portugal
job_description: Join the transformation of global shared services from our new GBS hub in Portugal!Your new Company:A global industrial company is expanding its Global Business Services (GBS) operations and establishing a regional GBS satellite in Portugal to support Europe operations.This is your opportunity to join at the ground level and help shape GBS from the inside out!Your new Role:As an O2C Senior Accountant, you will carry out Sales and Billing activities, including working with internal and external customers in the billing process to guarantee accuracy, maximise the collection of cash and reduce overdue debt.Your responsibilities will include:In design and transition phase: support and document O2C processes as per defined methodology.Undertake order management (Process sales order, Chase project managers and administrators to update project).Create and distribute invoices.Reporting: e.g. outstanding & overdue reports (Services).Manage dunning and cash collection tasks including dispute.Supporting employee onboarding and training.Identifying and implementing tactical performance improvements.Being a subject matter expert including participating in project work as required.Complete sales ledger responsibility, including processing sales order, cash collection & sales ledger integrity.Achieve agreed collection targets.Ensure correct procedures and processes are in place as per credit policy, and chase payments from customers on a regular basis.Ensure that all business queries are resolved within agreed timeframes.Ensure all client queries are resolved within agreed timeframes.Take ownership of targets and objectives.What you will need to be successful:Accounting qualification would be a plus.2-5 years’ of Sales Administration or O2C experience. Experience of working in a finance environment; numerate with the ability to understand, interpret and communicate complex accounts and financial reports.Comprehensive knowledge of Order to Cash Process and procedure is essential, along with the ability to demonstrate excellent customer service skills.Strong command of verbal and written Spanish and English (mandatory);Excellent IT skills including Excel.Good systems knowledge, preferably experience working with SAP.Proven track record of achieving KPI’s.Deliver to strict guidelines and to tight deadlines.Must be able to demonstrate a high standard of communications skills, with the ability to identify the optimum communication channel as well as excellent customer service skills.Proactive, responsive and action orientated.An individual & team player meeting the demands of both.What the Company can offer you:Be at the heart of enabling global GBS operations;Work in a multicultural, global team environment;Shape the employee and customer experience agenda in a growing organization.

PwC Services Portugal (PSP) is a joint venture between PwC Luxembourg and PwC Portugal, serving as an integral extension of PwC Luxembourg. Our growing team operates as an acceleration centre supporting PwC Luxembourg’s clients. Leveraging a unique pool of talented professionals based in Matosinhos, our operating model ensures scalability, consistency, and quality in service delivery by integrating seamlessly into PwC Luxembourg’s operations and addressing talent attraction challenges sustainably.PSP provides a range of services, including Assurance Audit (spanning all industries, with a particular focus on Real Estate and Private Equity), Broader Assurance Services, Tax (including Managed Services, Corporate Tax, VAT, and Accounting), and Advisory (specializing in Consulting, Regulatory and Deals & Strategy). Applying generally accepted valuation principles, methodologies, and techniques in support of IPEV, IFRS, US GAAP and Lux GAAP. Performing valuation analysis of financial instruments and structured products. Creating independent financial models utilizing income or market approaches to estimate or assess the value of a business and the securities within its capital structure. Analyzing complex ideas or proposals and build a range of meaningful recommendations. Use multiple sources of information including broader stakeholder views to develop solutions and recommendations. Use data and insights to inform conclusions and support decision-making. Develop a point of view on key global trends, and how they impact clients. Manage a variety of viewpoints to build consensus and create positive outcomes for all parties. Simplify complex messages, highlighting and summarizing key points. Deliver exceptional results for our clients. Let's Talk About You. If You Have/are... Master's degree in finance/economics/accounting/engineering or similar. Strong communication and interpersonal skills with a perfect command of English (German and French are deemed as a strong asset, any other European language will be considered as valuable). Professional experience of 2-5 years of ideally from two to four years with a relevant exposure to Valuation and Modeling, preferably in the valuation/modelling advisory team of a Big Four firm, professional services firm (e.g., Mazars, BDO, Grant Thornton) and/or valuation specialist advisor (e.g., BHB Partners, Stout, VRC, Houlihan Lokey, Alvarez & Marsal, Accuracy, Eight Advisory). Candidates with a solid audit background in alternatives investments and/or in-house AIFM valuation function experience, would also be considered if they are proficient in English, German and French. As the Manager of Treasury, you will assist in managing the overall treasury function, driving strategic cash flow, liquidity, and risk management initiatives across the organization. This role requires superior leadership and decision-making skills to optimize treasury operations, guide strategic financial health, and provide high-level insights to the Head of Treasury (HOT) and senior management. You will be responsible for overseeing all daily, weekly, and monthly treasury operations and reporting, ensuring strict compliance with financial policies, and mentoring the treasury team.Key ResponsibilitiesI. Strategic Leadership & ManagementStrategic Treasury Oversight: Lead the design and execution of the company's treasury strategy, ensuring alignment with overall business goals and multi-currency operations.Team Leadership: Oversee the performance of the treasury team, managing task prioritization and serving as the primary decision-maker for complex treasury issues.HOD Partnership: Provide high-level assistance and strategic input to the Head of Department (HOD) in managing overall consolidated treasury tasks.II. Liquidity & Cash ManagementCash Flow Optimization: Direct the company's cash flow, liquidity, and risk management framework.Advanced Forecasting: Implement and refine advanced cash forecasting models and strategies to ensure optimal liquidity for operational needs.Process Improvement: Drive initiatives for significant enhancement and automation of the company’s cash management processes and systems.III. Risk & Foreign Exchange ManagementCurrency Risk Strategy: Develop and execute comprehensive currency risk management strategies, proactively assessing the impact of foreign exchange fluctuations on the company's financial standing.Cross-Border Operations: Oversee compliance and efficiency for all cross-border pay-ins and payouts, ensuring seamless transaction execution.Funding & Investment: Formulate and execute short-term funding strategies and investment policies to maximize returns while managing risk.IV. Relationship & Operational ManagementBanking Relations: Establish, monitor, and maintain high-level, effective relationships with banks and financial institutions to negotiate and optimize financial services, terms, and fees.Reporting & Compliance: Ensure timely, accurate, and compliant reporting for all remittance and merchants' ledger activities.Ad-hoc Initiatives: Undertake high-impact ad-hoc responsibilities and strategic projects as assigned, driving organizational flexibility and responsiveness.Eligibility CriteriaEducation: ICMA/ACCA/CA Qualified or a Master's degree in Finance or a related field from a reputed university is required.Experience: Minimum of 4-5 years of relevant experience within the financial services industry, with at least 3 years in a leadership/management capacityTechnical Proficiency: Expert proficiency in cash forecasting, cash management techniques, short-term funding strategies, currency risk management, and foreign exchange transactions.Skills: Exceptional analytical, problem-solving, and decision-making skills, with meticulous attention to detailsWork Ethic: Highly self-motivated, demonstrating ownership of critical projects, and the ability to work independently to deliver results under pressure.Communication: Strong interpersonal and communication skills, required for effective collaboration with cross-functional teams and senior external stakeholders

Patreon is a media and community platform where over 300,000 creators give their biggest fans access to exclusive work and experiences. We offer creators a variety of ways to engage with their fans and build a lasting business including: paid memberships, free memberships, community chats, live video, and selling to fans directly with one-time purchases.Ultimately our goal is simple: fund the creative class. And we're leaders in that space, with:$10 billion+ generated by creators since Patreon's inception100 million+ free memberships for fans who may not be ready to pay just yet, and25 million+ paid memberships on Patreon today.We're continuing to invest heavily in building the best creator platform with the best team in the creator economy and are looking for a Trust and Safety Porto Site Lead to support our mission.This role is based in Porto, Portugal and operates on a hybrid work model (in-office 2 days per week).About The TeamTrust & Safety keeps Patreon welcoming and fair so Creators and their Fans can connect and creativity can thrive. We set clear, easy-to-follow standards and act quickly and consistently when something’s off, giving creators and fans confidence to create, share, and participate with respect across our global community.Our Porto site is our safety heartbeat in Europe: most of our frontline Trust & Safety work happens here. It’s a real community where people support each other, share context, and carry tough work together. Porto is also a source of honest insight -- feedback from the site has shaped policy decisions, tooling asks, and how we scale with people, BPOs, and automation. As Patreon becomes more public and discovery-led, Porto’s experience is central to how we evolve.About The RoleWe’re hiring a Trust & Safety Site Lead in our Porto location to keep the safety pipeline healthy during EU hours and make sure work moves seamlessly between Porto and the U.S. You’ll be Porto’s advocate for safety operations -- turning local signals into global action, helping teams adopt improvements that stick, and explaining what changed and why in plain language.You’ll sit in Trust & Safety so you have real operational context, but your remit is site-wide: chairing the Porto Leadership Council, partnering with the Admin/HR partner, and helping represent the needs of all Porto teams (not just T&S) in big decisions. You’ll lead through influence at first (no direct reports initially, with dotted-line relationships) and report to the Director of Trust & Safety Operations (based in the U.S.).As the site and product evolve, there is room for this role to grow into a broader people-lead scope based on business need and alignment with our leveling framework.About YouSignificant experience (typically 8+ years) in Trust & Safety, risk, operations, or a related field, including site or regional leadership responsibilityExperience leading teams or sites in the EU/UK regionProven track record of improving operational performance using data and structured problem solvingExperience working with or managing vendors/BPOsStrong communication skills in English and Portuguese, both written and verbalComfort operating in a global, matrixed environment and leading through influence as well as direct managementFamiliarity with EU/UK online safety expectations and how they translate into day-to-day operationsPassion for creators, communities, and the health of online ecosystemsAbout PatreonPatreon powers creators to do what they love and get paid by the people who love what they do. Our team is passionate about making this mission and our core values come to life every day in our work. Through this work, our Patronauts:Put Creators First | They’re the reason we’re here. When creators win, we win.Build with Craft | We sign our name to every deliverable, just like the creators we serve.Make it Happen | We don’t quit. We learn and deliver.Win Together | We grow as individuals. We win as a team.We hire talented and passionate people from different backgrounds because workplace diversity and inclusion is critical to our ability to serve creators worldwide. If you’re excited about a role but your past experience doesn’t match with every bullet point outlined above, we strongly encourage you to apply anyway. If you’re a creator at heart, are energized by our mission, and share our company values, we’d love to hear from you.Patreon is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We provide employment opportunities without regard to age, race, color, ancestry, national origin, religion, disability, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, veteran status, or any other protected class. If you need a reasonable accommodation during the interview process, please let us know via email at accommodations@patreon.Patreon offers a competitive benefits package including and not limited to salary, equity plans, healthcare, vacation leave, company holidays and recharge days, lifestyle stipends, learning and development sti

job_title: Closing Controls & Certification Team Manager | Finance
job_description: Company DescriptionNatixis in Portugal is a Centre of Expertise whose mission is to transform traditional banking by developing innovative solutions for the business, operations and work culture of Groupe BPCE worldwide.As part of Groupe BPCE’s international division, Natixis in Portugal designs and delivers solutions for its two core areas – Corporate & Investment Banking and Asset & Wealth Management – as well as transversal services that support all entities across the Group.With more than 3,000 employees representing 46 nationalities, the teams work across Information Technology, Banking Support Activities, and Compliance, in an integrated, inclusive, and cross-functional way, supporting all business lines and platforms of the Group.A disruptive mindset and a culture of proximity and agility identify Natixis in Portugal Team and reflect the company's mission to transform traditional banking at a global scale: a perfect match in the Portuguese dynamics and entrepreneurial ecosystem.Job DescriptionAs the Team Manager of Closing Controls & Certification (CC&C), you will be responsible for managing four teams, each led by a Team Leader (totaling 35 people). You will oversee the execution of closing controls and certifications for two teams in global markets (Equity Market and Fixed Income, Treasury & ALM), one dedicated financing scope (CC&C Financing), and one international team combining the APAC and US scope. You will play a key role in optimizing closing processes and ensuring the quality and integrity of financial data according to FDI (Financial Data Integrity) standards.ResponsibilitiesTeam Management: Supervise the Closing Controls & Certification Team Leaders (four), ensuring effective performance management and providing support for the professional development of each team member.Collaboration with Head Office and Finance Teams: Work closely with the Head Office in Paris and the Finance teams from the US and APAC platforms to coordinate activities, procedures, and controls within the CC&C teams.Recruitment and Integration: Manage the recruitment process in collaboration with functional leaders in Paris, ensuring an effective onboarding and training program for new team members.Resource Allocation: Organize and optimize the allocation of resources necessary for monthly closing tasks, ensuring each team is adequately equipped.Process Control and Optimization: Monitor and enhance the control framework throughout the monthly closing process, ensuring data accuracy and compliance with internal standards.Process Automation: Identify and aligne with Team Leaders to procede with automation solutions aimed at improving operational efficiency, reducing processing times and minimizing errors.Support for Reconciliations: Oversee monthly closings and, when necessary, assist teams in reconciling accounting data to ensure accuracy and reliability.Inter-team Interactions: Collaborate with teams from FDI (Financial Data Integrity – Data sourcing and DATR 1.2 controls ), FPC (LOD2) Market Risk, and Finance teams such as MC&R (Metrics Calculation & Reporting) and S&CA (Statutory & Consolidated Accounting), as well as with GMO (Global Markets Operations) and GBO (Global Banking Operations) to ensure smooth communication and coherence in all closing processes.Trend Monitoring: Identify and monitor key trends in the industry to implement best practices and maintain financial data integrity.Talent Management: Oversee talent management processes, including performance reviews and training programs, focusing on developing team capabilities.Budget Management: Monitor and manage the budget for your team, ensuring resources are utilized effectively and expenditures align with strategic goals.QualificationsEducation: Master’s degree, MBA, or equivalent in finance, accounting, or a related field.Experience: Ideally 4 years of team management experience in a financial environment, with expertise in financial control, auditing, or accounting.Technical Skills: Strong mastery of accounting principles (IFRS standards). Knowledge of financial products and accounting for these products is essential to provide support to the teams. Certification as a Certified Accountant or in progress towards obtaining this certification is a plus.Organizational Skills: Excellent organizational skills to effectively manage multiple teams and projects simultaneously.Leadership: Proven ability to inspire and motivate multicultural teams, ensuring personal and professional development of team members.Communication Skills: Excellent interpersonal skills to effectively interact with various stakeholders and engage stakeholders at all levels of the organization.Additional InformationOur workplace reflects the vibrant spirit of our locations, with initiatives such as a Green Transportation Budget, electric bikes and a flexible Hybrid Work Policy. job_title: Projektleiter Automation (m/w/d) - befristet auf 3 Jahre - full remote möglich
job_description: Ihre Rolle in unserem TeamSie übernehmen die internationale Fachprojektleitung Automatisierung E&AT (Elektrokonstruktion und Automatisierung). Innerhalb dieses vielfältigen Tätigkeitsgebietes sind Sie verantwortlich für die Koordination und/ oder Erstellung des Arbeitspakets E&AT bestehend aus Hardware, Visualisierung und SPS-Software. Darüber hinaus prüfen Sie die Lastenhefte in der Angebotsphase und Erstellen den Pflichtenhefteanteil E&AT für die Projektausführung. Sie koordinieren die Montage- und lnbetriebnahmeeinsätze im Automatisierungsbereich und übernehmen die fachliche Überwachung und Kontrolle der Anlagentests. Ihr ProfilSie bringen das notwendige Fachwissen auf Basis eines abgeschlossenen Studiums (Elektrotechnik, Automatisierungstechnik, Mechatronik) mit oder verfügen über eine vergleichbare Qualifikation/ Weiterbildung. Sie verfügen bereits über mehrjährige Berufserfahrung als Projektleiter, vorzugsweise aus der Logistikbranche. Gute Kenntnisse in der Antriebstechnik und Bus-Systemen. Sie besitzen eine lösungsorientierte Arbeitsweise und sind ein Teamplayer. Sie sind interessiert, flexibel und motiviert. Sie verfügen über sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift. Sie bringen die Bereitschaft mit, im In- und Ausland zu reisen (ca.30%/Jahr). Ihr Arbeitsumfeld bei Körber Supply Chain AutomationDie Körber Supply Chain Automation mit Hauptsitz in Leingarten und weiteren Standorten in Dahn, Rottendorf, Markdorf & Schwarzenbek ist Generalunternehmer für automatisierte Intralogistik-Systeme und dynamische Automatisierungsprojekte. Wir bieten unseren Kunden Beratung, Planung, Softwareentwicklung, Projektrealisierung, Service und Wartung - alles aus einer Hand. Die Bandbreite an Referenzen reicht von einfachen bis zu hochkomplexen Intralogistikprojekten in den unterschiedlichsten Branchen.Ihre VorteileWir verbinden die Vorteile eines mittelständischen Unternehmens mit den Vorteilen eines Großkonzerns:Gesicherter Arbeitsplatz in einer wachsenden, zukunftsorientierten Branche. Steuerfreier Sachbezugswert für den vielfältigen Kauf von Waren oder Dienstleistungen, vergünstigter Mittagstisch. „New way of working“ mit umfangreicher mobiler Arbeit möglich. Moderne IT-Ausstattung am Arbeitsplatz. Monatliche Mitarbeiterangebote über ein Rabattportal. Betriebliche Altersversorgung, Urlaubs- und Weihnachtsgeld, Sonderurlaub, Jubiläumszahlungen. 30 Tage work abroad pro Kalenderjahr innerhalb Europas möglich. Business Bike – auch für ein weiteres Familienmitglied. Jetzt bewerben und Teil unseres Teams werden!Bereit für den nächsten Schritt? Mit einem Klick auf „Jetzt bewerben“ kann es direkt losgehen! Solange die Position ausgeschrieben ist, ist eine Bewerbung jederzeit möglich.Wir wertschätzen Vielfalt und begrüßen daher alle Bewerbungen – unabhängig von Geschlecht, Nationalität, ethnischer und sozialer Herkunft, Religion/ Weltanschauung, Behinderung, Alter sowie sexueller Orientierung und Identität. Mehr zu unserer Kultur gibt es hier zu erfahren Kultur bei Körber - Vorbeischauen lohnt sich!Bei Fragen oder technischen Problemen stehen wir gerne telefonisch unter +49 40 21107 291 oder per E-Mail an jobs@koerber.com zur Verfügung. Sina Prinz ist die zuständige Ansprechperson für diese Position. Wir arbeiten mit festen Partnern zusammen und bitten daher Personalberatungen, von einer Kontaktaufnahme per Mail oder Telefon abzusehen.

Sword Health is shifting healthcare from human-first to AI-first through its AI Care platform, making world-class healthcare available anytime, anywhere, while significantly reducing costs for payers, self-insured employers, national health systems, and other healthcare organizations. Sword began by reinventing pain care with AI at its core, and has since expanded into women’s health, movement health, and more recently mental health. Since 2020, more than 700,000 members across three continents have completed 10 million AI sessions, helping Sword's 1,000+ enterprise clients avoid over $1 billion in unnecessary healthcare costs. Backed by 42 clinical studies and over 44 patents, Sword Health has raised more than $500 million from leading investors, including Khosla Ventures, General Catalyst, Transformation Capital, and Founders Fund. Learn more at www.swordhealth.com.We are seeking an experienced Senior Accountant – General Ledger to play a critical role in the monthly close and financial reporting process. This role is responsible for executing and reviewing core GL activities, ensuring balance sheet integrity, and supporting consolidated reporting in a multi-entity, international environment.The Senior Accountant – GL is a key pillar of the close engine and works closely with the Assistant Controller to deliver a clean, predictable T+5 close.What you’ll be doing:Execute core month-end GL close activities, including accruals, deferrals, and adjustmentsPrepare and review balance sheet reconciliationsEnsure completeness and accuracy of the general ledgerPerform FX remeasurement and related postingsAssist with group consolidation activities, including review of consolidation entries and FX impactsPrepare variance analysis and support management reportingSupport audits by preparing schedules and responding to auditor queriesEnforce accounting cutoffs, standards, and close disciplineWhat you need to have:Portuguese Chartered Accountant - Preferred5+ years of progressive accounting experienceProven experience in GL close ownership or senior close executionStrong understanding of IFRS and/or US GAAPExperience in multi-entity and multi-currency environmentsExposure to group consolidation concepts (eliminations, FX)Strong attention to detail with a reviewer mindsetAbility to operate effectively under close deadlinesSystems & Tools (Strong Plus): NetSuite experience (GL, intercompany, reporting); Experience with close management and reconciliation tools; Advanced Excel / reporting skillsTo ensure you feel good solving a big Human problem, we offer: A stimulating, fast-paced environment with lots of room for creativityA bright future at a promising high-tech startup companyCareer development and growth, with a competitive salaryThe opportunity to work with a talented team and to add real value to an innovative solution with the potential to change the future of healthcareA flexible environment where you can control your hours (remotely) with unlimited vacationAccess to our health and well-being program (digital therapist sessions)Remote or Hybrid work policyPortugal - Sword Benefits & Perks: Health, dental and vision insurance Meal allowance Equity shares Remote work allowance Flexible working hours Work from home Discretionary vacation Snacks and beverages English classNote: Please note that this position does not offer relocation assistance. Candidates must possess a valid EU visa and be based in Portugal.Sword Health complies with applicable Federal and State civil rights laws and does not discriminate on the basis of Age, Ancestry, Color, Citizenship, Gender, Gender expression, Gender identity, Gender information, Marital status, Medical condition, National origin, Physical or mental disability, Pregnancy, Race, Religion, Caste, Sexual orientation, and Veteran status.This range includes base, variable and equityThese compensation bands are just the starting point. Banking Support Activities, and Compliance, in an integrated, inclusive, and cross-functional way, supporting all business lines and platforms of the Group.A disruptive mindset and a culture of proximity and agility identify Natixis in Portugal Team and reflect the company's mission to transform traditional banking at a global scale: a perfect match in the Portuguese dynamics and entrepreneurial ecosystem.Job DescriptionGeneric Responsibilities: Produces regulatory reports on a timely basis, including COREP templates (such as Leverage ratio, Own funds, Credit and Market risks, PVA), RUBA templates on individual accounting level, Anacredit and Anatitres granular reportings, financial communication material, and other ad hoc reporting requirements (such as Loantape, AQR, Deep dive), to ensure compliance with regulatory requirements.Completes first level controls during production periods by performing checks on data and reports to ensure that they are accurate and complete, in order to minimize the risk of errors in regulatory reports.Completes the control framework during quarter-end closings as well as monthly closings by verifying the accuracy and completeness of data and reports, in order to ensure that all regulatory reporting requirements are met.Conducts case-by-case analysis required by internal and external stakeholders, such as Risk Department, Joint Supervisory Team, Groupe BPCE, etc., by reviewing data and reports and analyzing any discrepancies or issues, in order to address concerns and ensure compliance with regulatory requirements.Administers and enhance the business intelligence tools, such as Power BI and Tableau Software, by developing and maintaining reports, dashboards, and other tools to support regulatory reporting activities, in order to improve efficiency and effectiveness of reporting processes.Drafts and updates procedures and operating methods by identifying areas where improvements can be made and developing new procedures and methods to improve the accuracy, completeness, and timeliness of regulatory reports, in order to ensure compliance with regulatory requirements and best practices.Performs case-by-case analysis and answers questions required by internal and external stakeholders, such as the Finance or Risk Department, Audit or FPC, Joint Supervisory Team, and Groupe BPCE, to provide in-depth analysis and insights.Specific ResponsibilitiesSupports more junior Officers by sharing knowledge, providing technical guidance, and providing constructive feedback to foster a collaborative and inclusive environment that promotes learning and growth.QualificationsMaster’s Degree from a Business School or University closely related to the Finance industry;Management;Solid understanding of Prudential Frameworks (Basel III, CRR…);Strong knowledge of reporting requirements;European Banking Regulatory Reporting;Experience working in Risk Management;Previous Banking or Financial Industry Experience with similar Regulatory reporting set up;Data set manipulation;Power BI;MS Office;Fluent in English and French as a big plus.Soft SkillsResult oriented;Team spirit & transversality;Proactive;Curiosity and autonomous thinking;Kindness & humanity;Innovation.Additional InformationOur workplace reflects the vibrant spirit of our locations, with initiatives such as a Green Transportation Budget, electric bikes and a flexible Hybrid Work Policy. We promote wellbeing through the Honolulu Wellness Club, a Prayer Room, a Lactation Room, and themed Villages that inspire creativity and collaboration. Through our ESG and DEI strategies, we are committed to being inclusive, caring, and fair, ensuring every voice is heard and valued.

job_title: International Financial Reporting Controller - Fluent in English - All Genders - Permanent Contract - Hybrid
job_description: Join us at BNP Paribas Cardif, the Insurer for a Changing World.At BNP Paribas Cardif, we're not just an insurance provider; we're pioneers shaping the future of the industry. As the global leader in the insurance market, with a significant presence in savings and protection insurance across 30+ countries all over the globe, we're committed to innovation and excellence.At our European Service Center - Cardif Support, based in both Porto and Lisbon, Portugal, we're a vibrant hub of over 600 multicultural professionals speaking more than 11 languages!Our goal? To deliver the highest level of service, reliability, and efficiency to our clients, both internal and external, by offering diverse processes and services aligned with the insurance industry's best practices.Ready to kick off your next professional chapter with us?Become our newInternational Financial Reporting Controllerbased in PortoBe part of our dynamic International Financial Control Team (IFC), in charge of the production of the financial statements, but not only, with a permanent care for transversality with the other departments or Services of the Accounting Division (e.g. France Accounting, Consolidation) or the other Finance Divisions of BNP PARIBAS CARDIF Group.YOUR FUTURE MISSION IN THE IFC TEAMYour Key Responsibilities Will BeEnsure that the IFRS consolidation package is delivered within the fixed delays and in accordance with the applicable regulation and standards;Guarantee the reliability of the reporting processes;Reinforce the production of the various exhibits and financial appendices within the Group;Guarantee a regular reporting on your activities to your team and Manager (IFC);Contribute to the correct execution and the proper solving of complex reporting issues (incoming entities, mergers, and acquisitions, activities ceded…) during the quarterly reporting.READY TO MAKE AN IMPACT? HERE’S WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR:Knowledge of IFRS Standards, ideally for insurance, experience in the banking industry is a plus3 to 5 years in a financial accounting team, management controlling team or an accounting audit firm (Big 4) with the willingness to continue growing in that accounting and consolidation fieldEnglish (fluent), French is an assetAdaptability and ability to collaborateMotivated by new challenges, strive to be consistently learning and improving, efficiency and automatizations oriented.WHAT’S IN IT FOR YOU?In addition to our attractive salary, here’s a glimpse of what you can expect from us:Generous Perks: Enjoy meal, child and monthly flexible benefits allowances as part of our comprehensive benefits package.Comprehensive Coverage: Rest easy with our top-notch insurance package (health, life, and travel), applicable to your spouse and descendants.Flexibility Redefined: Embrace our hybrid working model for a perfect balance of commitment and personal flexibility.Endless and tailored Professional Growth: Fuel your career aspirations with continuous development opportunities on both local and global scalesVibrant Work Environment: Immerse yourself in our modern amenities, including zen rooms, free language courses, and diverse company events.Culture of Care: Be part of a multicultural environment that prioritizes employee wellbeing and professional growth.DIVERSITY AND INCLUSION ARE IMPORTANT TO US!BNP Paribas Cardif is an equal opportunity employer and proud to provide equal employment opportunity to all job seekers. We are actively committed to ensuring that no individual is discriminated against on the grounds of age, disability, gender reassignment, marriage or civil partnership status, pregnancy and maternity/paternity, race, religion or belief, sex or sexual orientation. Equity and diversity are at the core of our recruitment policy because we believe that they foster creativity and efficiency, which in turn increase performance and productivity. We strive to reflect the society we live in, while keeping with the image of our clients.CAN WE COUNT ON YOU?If you're ready to shape the future of insurance and your profile aligns with our vision, send us your application with CV, cover letter, and Certificate of Studies. We look forward to welcoming you to our team!Note: Only complete applications with all the required documentation will be considered.

job_title: Finance & Comptabilité - Candidatures Spontanées - Porto - H/F/X - Hybride
job_description: Rejoignez BNP Paribas Cardif, l'Assureur pour un Monde qui change.Chez BNP Paribas Cardif, nous ne sommes pas seulement un fournisseur d'assurances ; nous sommes des pionniers qui façonnent l'avenir de l'industrie. En tant que leader mondial sur le marché de l'assurance, avec une présence significative dans l'assurance d'épargne et de la protection dans plus de 30 pays à travers le monde, nous nous engageons dans l'innovation et l'excellence.Au sein de notre Centre de Services Européen - Cardif Support, basé à Porto et Lisbonne, au Portugal, nous formons un centre dynamique de plus de 480 professionnels multiculturels parlant 6 langues.Notre objectif ? Offrir un service d’excellence, de fiabilité et d'efficacité à nos clients, internes et externes, en proposant des processus et services diversifiés alignés sur les meilleures pratiques de l'industrie de l'assurance.Nous sommes à la recherche de candidatures spontanées de professionnels francophones (niveau de français B2 minimum), compétents et dynamiques dans les domaines de la finance ou comptabilité, pour rejoindre notre département à Porto !Compte tenu de nos projets ambitieux de développement et d’expansion de notre département financier, prévus pour 2026 et les années suivantes, nous encourageons les candidatures pour les spécialités suivantes :Reporting financierComptabilité (générale, transactionnelle, technique, rapprochements bancaires, etc.)Contrôle de gestionContrôle interne financierContrôle financier internationalConsolidation financièreComptabilité immobilièreAudit financièrePourquoi nous rejoindre ?En plus d'un salaire attractif, voici tout ce que nous vous offrons :Bénéfices Généreux: Profitez d'allocations pour les repas, le transport dans le cadre de notre package complet d'avantages. Couverture Complète: Soyez rassuré avec notre excellent package d'assurance (santé, vie et voyage), applicable à votre conjoint et enfant(s)Flexibilité Redéfinie: Adoptez notre modèle de travail hybride pour un équilibre parfait entre engagement et flexibilité personnelle.Croissance Professionnelle Infinie: Nourrissez vos aspirations professionnelles avec des opportunités de développement continu à l'échelle locale et mondiale.Environnement de Travail Dynamique: Plongez dans nos installations modernes, incluant des salles zen, des cours de langue gratuits et divers événements d'entreprise.Culture de Bien-être: Faites partie d'un environnement multiculturel qui priorise le bien-être des employés et la croissance professionnelle. LA DIVERSITÉ ET L'INCLUSION SONT IMPORTANTES POUR NOUS !BNP Paribas Cardif est un employeur garantissant l'égalité des chances et fier de fournir des opportunités d'emploi égales à tous les candidats. Nous nous engageons activement à garantir qu'aucun individu ne soit discriminé en raison de son âge, de son handicap, de sa réassignation de genre, de son statut matrimonial ou de partenariat civil, de sa grossesse et maternité/paternité, de sa race, de sa religion ou de ses croyances, de son sexe ou de son orientation sexuelle. L'équité et la diversité sont au cœur de notre politique de recrutement, car nous croyons qu'elles favorisent la créativité et l'efficacité, qui à leur tour augmentent les performances et la productivité. Nous nous efforçons de refléter la société dans laquelle nous vivons, tout en restant fidèles à l'image de nos clients.AVEZ-VOUS CE QU'IL FAUT?Si vous êtes prêt à façonner l'avenir de l'assurance et que votre profil correspond à notre vision, envoyez-nous votre candidature avec CV, lettre de motivation et certificat d'études. Nous avons hâte de vous accueillir dans notre équipe !Note : Seules les candidatures complètes avec tous les documents requis seront prises en considération.

Sword Health is shifting healthcare from human-first to AI-first through its AI Care platform, making world-class healthcare available anytime, anywhere, while significantly reducing costs for payers, self-insured employers, national health systems, and other healthcare organizations. Sword began by reinventing pain care with AI at its core, and has since expanded into women’s health, movement health, and more recently mental health. Since 2020, more than 700,000 members across three continents have completed 10 million AI sessions, helping Sword's 1,000+ enterprise clients avoid over $1 billion in unnecessary healthcare costs. Backed by 42 clinical studies and over 44 patents, Sword Health has raised more than $500 million from leading investors, including Khosla Ventures, General Catalyst, Transformation Capital, and Founders Fund. Learn more at www.swordhealth.com.We are seeking an experienced AP Lead to own the global procure-to-pay (P2P) process in a centralized, multi-entity environment. This role is responsible for AP execution quality, invoice throughput, payment accuracy, tax correctness, and AP-related close discipline, with a strong emphasis on system-enabled efficiency and cross-functional collaboration.The AP Lead is a key partner to Accounting and FP&A, ensuring accurate departmental allocations, clean accruals, and proper budget alignment within P2P systems to support a predictable T+5 monthly close.What you’ll be doing:Own and manage the end-to-end accounts payable and P2P processLead and develop AP Specialists and TraineesEnforce invoice, expense, and cutoff discipline to support a T+5 closeOversee invoice processing, coding, approvals, and postingReview and approve payment runsPrepare and review AP accruals in partnership with FP&AEnsure accurate department and cost center allocationsSupport budget integration and controls within P2P systemsOwn vendor master data governanceEnsure correct VAT treatment, with a strong focus on Portuguese tax requirementsResolve escalated vendor, allocation, and tax-related issuesPartner with Accounting, FP&A, and Systems teams on continuous improvement initiativesWhat you need to have:Portuguese Chartered Accountant — strongly preferred 5-7+ years of AP or accounting operations experienceStrong knowledge of Portuguese VAT and local tax requirementsProven experience leading AP operations in a centralized environmentDemonstrated ability to partner effectively with FP&AExperience in multi-entity and/or international organizationsStrong process mindset with attention to detail and controlsComfortable operating in high-volume transaction environmentsSystems & Implementation Experience (Strong Plus)NetSuite AP experience (vendor master, invoice processing, approvals, payments)Hands-on participation in an AP / P2P system implementation, such as: Tipalti, Stampli, Coupa, ZIP or similar. Experience with workflow and close coordination tools (e.g., FloQast)Exposure to budget controls, coding rules, or validation logic within P2P systemsTo ensure you feel good solving a big Human problem, we offer:A stimulating, fast-paced environment with lots of room for creativityA bright future at a promising high-tech startup companyCareer development and growth, with a competitive salaryThe opportunity to work with a talented team and to add real value to an innovative solution with the potential to change the future of healthcareA flexible environment where you can control your hours (remotely) with unlimited vacationAccess to our health and well-being program (digital therapist sessions)Remote or Hybrid work policyPortugal - Sword Benefits & Perks: Health, dental and vision insurance Meal allowance Equity shares Remote work allowance Flexible working hours Work from home Discretionary vacation Snacks and beverages English classNote: Please note that this position does not offer relocation assistance. Candidates must possess a valid EU visa and be based in Portugal.Sword Health complies with applicable Federal and State civil rights laws and does not discriminate on the basis of Age, Ancestry, Color, Citizenship, Gender, Gender expression, Gender identity, Gender information, Marital status, Medical condition, National origin, Physical or mental disability, Pregnancy, Race, Religion, Caste, Sexual orientation, and Veteran status.This range includes base, variable and equityThese compensation bands are just the starting point. Once someone joins and proves they’re outlier talent, we adjust quickly to ensure their compensation aligns with their impact.Our job titles may span more than one career level. Actual pay is determined by skills, qualifications, experience, location, market demand, and other factors. Compensation details listed in this posting reflect the base salary and any potential variable, bonus or sales incentives, and the Company’s estimation of the value of private company stock options, if applicable. Job DescriptionWho Are We:As part of Mantu’s growth, we are looking for a new talent within our finance team. Finance at Mantu gathers strategic advisors who drive economic performance. The team also provides economic analyses to support the decision-making process and define key strategies. Mantu's financial activities include accounting, treasury management and financing, investment strategy, procurement, and management control.Job Description:You will join our finance team, supporting the CFO and driving strategic financial initiatives across the organization. This role is pivotal in optimizing financial systems, leading cross-functional projects, and providing advanced analytics to support business decisions.Your MissionsBe responsible for a scope with several countries.Review and monitor the monthly P&L of the scope.Provide the analysis required to establish a yearly budget and monitor its gap with the P&L.Provide financial analysis and forecasting.Analyze the profitability of individual business lines/projects and identify actions to preserve/improve the business’ margins.Support the production of financial reports to be submitted to the Board of Directors.Oversee and monitor the structured projects provisions.Manage cash and monitor the invoicing flows.Interact and coordinate with the other finance teams: treasury, controlling, accounting, invoicing, etc., and staff departments: HR, Management, and Director.Manage local FP&As and a team of business controller.Your ProfileGraduated from a Master's degree.Previous experience between 4-6 years in a similar position (corporate finance, controlling, fp&a analyst…) or in audit in a Big 4.Demonstrated proficiency with Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint presentations creation abilities along with financial modeling experience.Strong analytical and data gathering skills, with attention to detail.Excellent communication and inter-relational skills; ability to interact at all levels of the organization.Able to work in a fast-paced environment and manage multiple projects simultaneously.French and English fluent.What We OfferAn international community bringing together 110+ different nationalitiesAn environment where trust has a central place: 70% of our key leaders started their careers at the first level of responsibilitiesA robust training system with our internal Academy and 250+ available modulesA vibrant workplace that frequently gathers for internal events (afterworks, team buildings, etc.)At Mantu, sustainability is part of everything we do. You’ll have the opportunity to turn your ideas into action and make a tangible impact. Every day, our teams bring our ESG commitments to life, from reducing our footprint to driving positive change within our communities. Through our WeCare Together program, you’ll be empowered to design and lead projects that create real social or environmental impact, with the company’s full support.Mantu is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace. We are committed to promoting diversity within the workforce and creating an inclusive working environment. For this purpose, we welcome applications from all qualified candidates regardless of gender, sexual orientation, race, ethnicity, beliefs, age, marital status, disability, or other characteristics.Who are we?Mantu is an independent international consulting player, founded in 2007. Bringing together expert and complementary brands, Mantu stands out for the breadth of its spectrum, responding to all business transformation challenges.Its activities are divided into four practices:Leadership & Advocacy, Technology, Brand Strategy & Experiences and Total Talent Management.A wide range of skills, all serving a single mission: connecting and powering companies with leading teams and technology to succeed faster and sustainably. From its headquarters in Geneva, Switzerland, Mantu relies on a community of 12,000 talented people in more than 60 countries on 5 continents and has a turnover of 1billion euros.

and action plans.Proficiency in calculation and reporting tools, including advanced Excel, SQL, and BI platforms (Power BI, Tableau, or equivalent).Skilled in documenting and updating operational PwC Services Portugal (PSP) is a joint venture between PwC Luxembourg and PwC Portugal, serving as an integral extension of PwC Luxembourg. Our growing team operates as an acceleration center supporting PwC Luxembourg’s clients. Leveraging a unique pool of talented professionals based in Matosinhos, our operating model ensures scalability, consistency, and quality in service delivery by integrating seamlessly into PwC Luxembourg’s operations and addressing talent attraction challenges sustainably.PSP provides a range of services, including Assurance Audit (spanning all industries, with a particular focus on Real Estate and Private Equity), Broader Assurance Services, Tax (including Managed Services, Corporate Tax, VAT, and Accounting), and Advisory (specializing in the Global Fund Industry and Real Estate Valuation).Be a part of our team of solvers where you will (specific responsibilities include but are not limited to): Perform and carry out valuation analysis and reviews of a wide spectrum of assets (Private Equity, Private Debt; Infrastructure assets, as well as Intangible assets); Assess the reasonableness of valuations and financial models in the context of financial reporting and assurance related engagements; Have the opportunity for exposure to advisory engagements across the Alternatives industry such as valuation, process implementation and review, data analytics and benchmarking; Benefit from our ‘Valuation Learning Pathway’, a unique and distinctive program that will allow you to grow as a valuation professional. About Let’s talk about you. If you are/have … 2 to 3 years of experience in a similar role; Advanced theoretical and practical knowledge of valuation principles and techniques (income, market, and asset based approach) with regards to business valuation; Strong analytical skills, problem solver, detail-oriented, entrepreneurial, autonomous and enjoying working in a multi-cultural environment; Ability to research and analyse pertinent client, industry, and financial matters, while demonstrating strong technical and analytical skills; Being flexible when adapting to changing client needs and market conditions; Ability to manage multiple threads of tasks and / or engagements; Proven ability to mentor and guide junior team members, and contribute to a collaborative team environment; Sound knowledge of recognized valuation guidelines, such as IPEV guidelines or IVS; Sound knowledge of industry software’s and tools (i.e. Bloomberg, S&P CapIQ, and/or advanced Excel modelling); Knowledge of option pricing models; Fluency in English, both written and verbally. …You are the candidate we are looking for!

Farfetch is a leading global marketplace for the luxury fashion industry, connecting customers in over 190 countries with items from more than 50 countries and over 1,400 of the world’s best brands and charming boutiques from around the world. Farfetch opens a world of luxury for endless expressions of style, delivering a truly unique shopping experience and access to the most extensive selection of luxury on a global marketplace.FINANCEThe Finance team stands as a strategic pillar of our organization, enabling the delivery of our business and financial goals, ensuring robust financial controls and optimizing overall financial performance. Finance is responsible for managing capital, ensuring financial integrity, optimizing resource allocation, and delivering accurate reporting to drive sustainable growth and profitability.PORTOOur office is near Porto, in the north of Portugal, and is located in a vibrant business hub. It offers a dynamic and welcoming environment where our employees can connect and network with a large community of tech professionals.THE ROLEReporting to the Financial Reporting Manager, you will drive the required process and policy amendments that are necessary for prompt, worldwide and smooth execution on the standards. You will work jointly with the accounting coordinators reporting and accounting matters. You will have an important role for the accountancy of our company, working together with the whole team to achieve the goals for the monthly closing.What You'll DoPrepares booking of assets, liabilities, revenues and expenses entries by compiling and analysing accounting information;Analyses the reasonability of trial balances’ variances;Analyses the consolidated information for the group, namely its Balance Sheet and P&L Fluxes, providing support in the financial statements’ disclosures;Complies with legal requirements;Participates in meetings and guarantees appropriate progress;Prepares new accounting policies and procedures for the group;Offers support to colleagues in specific accounting operations;Guarantees that controls are carried out , recorded and are working efficiently and promptly;Provides support on the external auditWho You AreA bachelor or master degree in accounting, management, economics or auditing;At least 3 years of functional experience in areas of accounting, auditing or consolidation;Knowledge of IFRS, US Gaap knowledge is a plus;Previous Experience in Consolidation either from a preparer or auditor’s point of view is a plusStrong drive for results, including proactive problem resolution skills in delivering stringent deadlines;Proficiency in conveying information efficiently and across departments;Outstanding writing aptitude and capability to organize presentations and papers;Fluent in English, both written and spoken is mandatory;An Excellent team player with the ability to interact with different departments;Considerable knowledge of spreadsheet software and basic computer systems, Microsoft AX and SAP BPC will be a plus; Demonstrated ability to perform accurate numeric calculations;An Excellent communicator;Dynamic, with high sense of responsibility and autonomyRewards & BenefitsHealth insurance for the whole family, flexible working environment and well-being support and toolsExtra days off, sabbatical program and days for you to give back for the communityTraining opportunities and free access to UdemyFlexible benefits programEQUAL OPPORTUNITIES STATEMENT Farfetch is an equal opportunities employer ensuring that all applicants are treated equally and fairly throughout our recruitment process. We are committed to fostering a dynamic, inclusive workplace that values diversity, where creativity and collaboration thrive. PwC Services Portugal (PSP) is a joint venture between PwC Luxembourg and PwC Portugal, serving as an integral extension of PwC Luxembourg. Our growing team operates as an acceleration centre supporting PwC Luxembourg’s clients. Leveraging a unique pool of talented professionals based in Matosinhos, our operating model ensures scalability, consistency, and quality in service delivery by integrating seamlessly into PwC Luxembourg’s operations and addressing talent attraction challenges sustainably.PSP provides a range of services, including Assurance Audit (spanning all industries, with a particular focus on Real Estate and Private Equity), Broader Assurance Services, Tax (including Managed Services, Corporate Tax, VAT, and Accounting), and Advisory (specializing in Consulting, Regulatory and Deals & Strategy).For this opportunity, your CV must be submitted in English.Your MissionAs a Financial Due Diligence Analyst based in Porto, you will join PwC’s leading Deals and Strategy& platform, contributing to high-impact M&A engagements across Europe. Working closely alongside our Luxembourg Deals team, you will support the delivery of rigorous financial analyses and strategic insights that help clients make informed and confident investment decisions. In this role, you will dissect business performance, assess value drivers, identify risks and opportunities, and help shape the narrative behind the numbers.You will be part of a dynamic, high-performing team operating at the intersection of finance, strategy and execution, supporting transformational transactions across a wide range of industries. This position offers a fast-paced environment, a steep learning curve and significant exposure to complex cross-border mandates where your work will have a visible impact.Be a Part Of Our Team Where You Will Perform financial due diligence procedures, including analysis of historical performance, quality of earnings, working capital trends, cash flows and net debt positions. Support the review of VDD reports and contribute to top-up analyses addressing specific client questions and scope areas. Prepare clear working papers, financial schedules, bridge analyses and summary outputs in line with PwC methodology and quality standards. Assist in the preparation of client deliverables (reports, slides, data books) with a focus on accuracy, clarity and business insight. Collaborate seamlessly with Luxembourg teams, engaging in regular calls, workflow coordination, and proactive communication on progress and issues. Support data extraction, cleansing and analysis from trial balances, general ledgers, management accounts and other financial datasets. Contribute to continuous improvement of tools, templates and automation initiatives (Alteryx/Python/Power BI, etc.) to enhance efficiency and analytical depth. Let’s Talk About You. If You Have … Degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, Business Administration or a related field. 1–3 years of experience in audit, financial analysis, transaction services, accounting, or a similar analytical role (Big-4 experience is an advantage). Strong understanding of financial statements, accounting principles (IFRS/LuxGAAP is a plus) and key M&A due diligence concepts. Solid analytical skills with the ability to interpret large datasets and identify trends, issues and insights. Advanced Excel skills; experience with Alteryx, Power BI, or Python is considered an asset. Excellent communication skills (spoken and written) in English; other European languages (French/German) are a plus. Ability to work collaboratively in remote, cross-border teams with strong attention to detail and a commitment to delivering high-quality work. …You are the candidate we are looking for!

stakeholders in an international environment

job_title: Analyste de Sécurité Financière – M/F/X – Lisbonne ou Porto – CDI – Hybride - Spontanée
job_description: Rejoignez BNP Paribas Cardif, l'Assureur pour un Monde qui change.Chez BNP Paribas Cardif, nous ne sommes pas seulement un fournisseur d'assurances ; nous sommes des pionniers qui façonnent l'avenir de l'industrie. En tant que leader mondial sur le marché de l'assurance, avec une présence significative dans l'assurance d'épargne et de la protection dans plus de 30 pays à travers le monde, nous nous engageons dans l'innovation et l'excellence.Au sein de notre Centre de Services Européen - Cardif Support, basé à Porto et Lisbonne, au Portugal, nous formons un centre dynamique de plus de 600 professionnels multiculturels parlant 6 langues.Notre objectif ? Offrir un service d’excellence, de fiabilité et d'efficacité à nos clients, internes et externes, en proposant des processus et services diversifiés alignés sur les meilleures pratiques de l'industrie de l'assurance.Prêt(e) à démarrer un nouveau chapitre professionnel avec nous ?Devenez notre nouvel Analyste de Sécurité Financière (M/F/X) basé(e) à Lisbonne ou PortoIntégrez notre équipe dynamique de Sécurité FinancièreL’équipe de Sécurité Financière (Niveau 2 de Conformité) a été créée en janvier 2022 et s’agrandit rapidement pour faire face aux enjeux de la sécurité financière de l’une des plus grandes banques mondiales. L’équipe dynamique, motivée et professionnelle s’assure que les tentatives de transactions illicites n’aboutissent pas. Si vous aimez l’investigation et l’analyse, si vous êtes responsable, avez un focus centré sur les résultats (priorité à la qualité !) et êtes impliqué en matière de Responsabilité Sociale, nous serions ravis de vous rencontrer !VOTRE FUTUR MISSION AU SEIN DE L’ÉQUIPE SECURITE FINANCIEREAssurer l’analyse de niveau 2 et la validation ou le refus des transactions financières escaladées par les analystes de conformité de niveau 1 ou par les équipes opérationnelles, selon les règles de délégation financière et les procédures établies Contribuer à alerter la hiérarchie en cas de détection d’une anomalie administrative, d’un risque opérationnel ou d’un dysfonctionnement qui pourrait affecter la qualité du service fourni au client interne comme au client final Assurer une attitude responsable, mature et professionnelle, via une bonne gestion du temps et des priorités et dans le respect des collègues et de la hiérarchiePRÊT(E) À FAIRE LA DIFFÉRENCE ? VOICI CE QUE NOUS RECHERCHONS :Une bonne connaissance du domaine de la Conformité (Compliance, Financial Crimes), des principales procédures afférentes ainsi que du cadre réglementaire (une précédente expérience dans le domaine de la Conformité est valorisée)D’excellentes capacités organisationnelles et de solides compétences analytiquesUne capacité à travailler de manière autonome et à délivrer les résultats tout en maintenant une haute qualité De l’ambition, des aptitudes d’apprentissage et un bon esprit d’équipe Une maîtrise du français professionnel, à l’écrit et à l’oral (C1-C2)Pourquoi nous rejoindre ?/ QUELS AVANTAGES POUR VOUS ?En plus d'un salaire attractif, voici tout ce que nous vous offrons :Bénéfices Généreux: Profitez d'allocations pour les repas, le transport dans le cadre de notre package complet d'avantages.Couverture Complète: Soyez rassuré avec notre excellent package d'assurance (santé, vie et voyage), applicable à votre conjoint et enfant(s)Flexibilité Redéfinie: Adoptez notre modèle de travail hybride pour un équilibre parfait entre engagement et flexibilité personnelle.Croissance Professionnelle Infinie: Nourrissez vos aspirations professionnelles avec des opportunités de développement continu à l'échelle locale et mondiale.Environnement de Travail Dynamique: Plongez dans nos installations modernes, incluant des salles zen, des cours de langue gratuits et divers événements d'entreprise.Culture de Bien-être: Faites partie d'un environnement multiculturel qui priorise le bien-être des employés et la croissance professionnelle.LA DIVERSITÉ ET L'INCLUSION SONT IMPORTANTES POUR NOUS !BNP Paribas Cardif est un employeur garantissant l'égalité des chances et fier de fournir des opportunités d'emploi égales à tous les candidats. Nous nous engageons activement à garantir qu'aucun individu ne soit discriminé en raison de son âge, de son handicap, de sa réassignation de genre, de son statut matrimonial ou de partenariat civil, de sa grossesse et maternité/paternité, de sa race, de sa religion ou de ses croyances, de son sexe ou de son orientation sexuelle. L'équité et la diversité sont au cœur de notre politique de recrutement, car nous croyons qu'elles favorisent la créativité et l'efficacité, qui à leur tour augmentent les performances et la productivité. Nous nous efforçons de refléter la société dans laquelle nous vivons, tout en restant fidèles à l'image de nos clients.AVEZ-VOUS CE QU’IL FAUT ?Si vous êtes prêt à façonner l'avenir de l'assurance et que votre profil correspond à notre vision, envoyez-nous votre candidature avec CV, lettre de motivation et certificat d'é

This is a hands-on role that combines strategic oversight, operational execution, and team leadership. You will ensure robust financial control, drive margin improvement, and support the business in achieving its growth objectives.Key ResponsibilitiesOwn the month-end close end-to-end, including postings, reconciliations, and balance sheet integrity.Coordinate accounting, management control, and reporting, ensuring timely and accurate updates to the CFO.Maintain tight cash and working capital recommendations.Lead margin control: track unit economics and contribution, validate cost allocations, identify leakage, and partner with Operations on fixes.Prepare budgets and periodic forecasts, supporting longer-term planning and scenario analysis.Ensure compliance with local tax obligations and statutory requirements, coordinating with external advisors as needed.Lead and develop the We proudly serve leading pharmaceutical companies and top-tier biotech firms across the U.S., Europe, and Japan. Our cutting-edge facilities are based in Porto, Portugal, where innovation and scientific excellence drive every project. For more information, please visit FairJourney Biologics (fjbio.com).The RoleAs an Accounting Manager, you will be responsible for ensuring the accuracy, integrity, and reliability of the company’s financial data across the entire group, which currently includes entities in Portugal, in UK and in USA. You will lead the group accounting function, implement and maintain strong internal controls, and drive continuous improvements in accounting processes and systems.This role requires deep technical accounting expertise, strong leadership capabilities, and a strategic mindset to support the organization’s financial health, compliance, and decision-making in a multi-entity, international environment.Key responsibilitiesLead, manage, and develop the group accounting function, ensuring effective team performance and operational excellence. Oversee all accounting operations for the group, ensuring accurate, timely, and compliant financial reporting across entities.Ensure consistency in accounting policies, procedures, and internal controls across the group, aligned with group standards and local statutory requirements.Manage, coordinate and review month-end, quarter-end, and year-end closing processes at group and local entity level.Prepare, review, and present group consolidated financial statements to senior leadership.Prepare year-end financial statements report at group and local entity level.Contribute to budget preparation, forecasting, and variance analysis at both group and entity level, in collaboration with Finance and business teams.Ensure compliance with applicable accounting standards (GAAP, IFRS), tax laws, and regulatory requirements.Manage relationships with external auditors, tax authorities, banks, and other financial institutions in all jurisdictions where the group operates.Provide financial insights and strategic guidance to senior management.Oversee cash flow management, balance sheet integrity, and financial risk assessment for the group.Strong background in implementing, improving, and optimizing accounting systems and internal controls.Experience leveraging automation, data analytics, or AI-driven tools is a must.Your ProfileBachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field; Master’s degree preferred.Certified Accountant in Portugal.8+ years of progressive accounting experience, including at least 3 years in a managerial or supervisory role in multi-entity or international environments.Proven experience leading accounting operations, including month-end, quarter-end, and year-end close processes.Strong leadership capabilities with a track record of developing high-performing accounting teams.Deep technical accounting expertise with solid knowledge of GAAP, IFRS, and statutory reporting requirements.Demonstrated experience designing, implementing, and maintaining accounting policies, procedures, and internal controls.Strong experience working with external auditors, tax authorities, and financial institutions.Solid understanding of cash flow management, balance sheet integrity, and financial risk assessment.Ability to partner with senior management by providing clear financial insights and supporting strategic decision-making.Proven ability to drive continuous improvement initiatives across accounting processes and systems.Strong project management skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities and deadlines in a fast-paced environment.High ethical standards, attention to detail, and commitment to data accuracy and financial integrity.Experience with accounting systems and ERP environments; exposure to automation, data analytics, or AI-enabled finance tools is a must.Fluency in written and spoken English is essential.We OfferIntegration in a highly motivated teamWorking in an international environmentEmployee benefits packageCareer developmentIf you're ready to take the next step in your career path, we’d love to hear from you.

At EY, you’ll have the chance to build a career as unique as you are, with the global scale, support, inclusive culture, and technology to become the best version of you. And we’re counting on your unique voice and perspective to help EY become even better, too. Join us and build an exceptional experience for yourself, and a better working world for all.The opportunityWe are looking to strengthen our IFRS team with experience profiles. Within this team we actively support our audit teams and our clients in auditing their IFRS consolidated financial statements. We provide accounting assistance to both internal and external clients on complex business transactions like M&A, IPO, or restructuring. We also organize and manage both internal and external IFRS events and trainings. We are currently looking to welcome an experienced profile within our team. Someone with hands-on approach that can assist in performing not only technical analysis, but also providing practical insight. Our IFRS team is actively contributing to the training of both colleagues and clients. Therefore, a part of your job would also be to provide such IFRS trainings internally as well as externally.Our IFRS and audit teams work with many of the world’s leading and fasters growing companies in a variety of industries.Through this vacant position we envision to recruit experienced profiles to work specifically on accounting excellence.Key ResponsibilitiesWithing our IFRS team you will act as an IFRS expert for our audit team and your client portfolio. You will have the following tasks & responsibilities: Acting as an expert regarding all IFRS related questions (related to a transaction or an audit). You ensure the follow-up of the IFRS developments & external publications to be able to advice clients correctly on the impact of these changes on their daily financial management. You support the audit teams with IFRS assignments. You review the financial reports of performed audits.Skills and attributes for success Analytical mindset. Attention to detail. A strong work ethic. Hands-on and pragmatic attitude. Focused on continuous improvement. Efficient and innovative. Creative, proactive, team player and critical thinking. Dedicated, innovative, resourceful, analytical, and able to work under pressure.To qualify for the role, you must have You have an Academic education in Economics, Finance, Accounting, or similar fields of study. You have at least 2 years of professional experience in a domain relevant to IFRS. You have a good knowledge and interest of IFRS standards and SNC. You have a passion for IFRS and are willing to continuously keep your knowledge up to date with the new evolvements. You are a results-oriented team player; and You have an upper intermediate English level and be fluent in Portuguese.Ideally, you’ll also have Knowledge in USGAAP. Interest on problem solving situations with autonomy to research and discuss potential solutions. Good technical writing and presentation skills.What We Look ForAn effective communicator and you’ll be a confident team player equipped with strong people management skills; Creative, proactive, a team player, and able to work under pressure. It’s important to have a strategically focused mindset and the ability to deliver multiple projects and present information in a clear and concise manner. Business mindset and some commercial acumen. We value a strong work ethic, forward-thinking, and being a self-starter; If you’re ready to take on a wide range of responsibilities and are committed to seeking out new ways to make a difference, this role is for you.What We OfferWe offer a competitive remuneration package where you’ll be rewarded for your individual and team performance. Plus, we offer support and coaching, opportunities to develop new skills and progress your career, and the freedom and flexibility to handle your role in a way that’s right for you. Continuous learning: You’ll develop the mindset and skills to navigate whatever comes next. Success as defined by you: We’ll provide the tools and flexibility, so you can make a meaningful impact, your way. Transformative leadership: We’ll give you the insights, coaching and confidence to be the leader the world needs.
